expectations.text: Output contains all 6 rounds (Round 1 through Round 6)
expectations.evidence: All 6 rounds present: Round 1 Growth Strategist Proposes through Round 6 CMO Decides

expectations.text: Growth Strategist, Brand Guardian, and CMO personas all appear and are distinct
expectations.evidence: Growth Strategist proposes/defends, Brand Guardian challenges/stress-tests, CMO decides and frames for CEO

expectations.text: Every challenge in Round 2 has a severity rating (critical/significant/minor)
expectations.evidence: All challenges have severity ratings in parentheses

expectations.text: Round 2 challenges are categorized (brand-risk, audience-assumption, channel-viability, etc.)
expectations.evidence: Categories present in brackets after severity ratings

expectations.text: Round 4 references specific mitigations from Round 3, not just repeating Round 2
expectations.evidence: Stress-tests attack specific defenses: misdiagnosis risk mitigation, enterprise brand contamination mitigation, etc.

expectations.text: Round 6 contains one of the 5 valid recommendations (approve, approve-with-conditions, pilot-first, defer, reject)
A CEO Summary section exists that is standalone and uses business language, not marketing jargon
expectations.evidence: CEO Summary present, uses revenue/risk/competitive language, not marketing jargon

expectations.text: Metrics table present with all 8 metrics filled in
expectations.evidence: Debate Metrics table with all 8 metrics filled

expectations.text: Budget impact section with original ask, revised ask (if changed), and expected ROI
expectations.evidence: Budget request stated in header ($1.2M phased), budget impact detailed in recommendation

expectations.text: Round 5 includes fallback plans for surviving challenges
expectations.evidence: Fallback plans included for each stress-test response
